text: And so, season 15 of Doctor Who came to
an end a few days ago, not with a bang,
but with the saddest and most pathetic
of all whimpers.
As Shooty Gatwa's 15th doctor went off
to that big old police box in the sky,
the BBC resorted to defrosting Billy
Piper of all people in a lastminute
desperate attempt to drum up something
vaguely resembling interest in this
desolate wasteland of a show. You know,
kind of like what they did by bringing
back David Tenant just to be humiliated
and belittled by politically approved
actors, only not as good or as
interesting. And it capped off what was
by far the most disastrously unpopular
season in the show's entire 60-year
history with some of the lowest rated
and least viewed episodes ever made, led
by a doctor so universally insufferable
that the actor himself bailed out after
just two seasons. Jesus, even Jodie
Whitaker managed to stick around for
three. Yeah, it was arguably three
seasons longer than she actually
deserved, but what the [ __ ] At least
she made it to the end. I mean, I
stopped actively participating in this
show after the crime against humanity
that was the Timeless Children. That
episode was the breaking point for me.
It destroyed any investment I ever had
in this absolute calamity. And to be
honest, that was probably a good thing.
You'd be amazed how liberating it is
when you just stop caring about
something. Ever since then, I've been
watching the self- emilation of Doctor
Who from a discrete distance with a kind
of sad detachment, like watching a
stranger's house burn down. and you
absolutely didn't have anything to do
with starting the fire. Well, not that
anyone can prove at
least. But the thing that amazes me
about all of this is that the BBC
actually expected anything different. I
mean, how completely [ __ ] out of
touch do you have to be to think that
you can spend years belittling,
insulting, and lecturing the very people
who watch your show and expect them to
keep coming back for more? What's that
you say? The villain this week is a
straight white male who has to be put in
his place by his ethnically and sexually
diverse moral superiors. Well, color me
shocked BBC. Where on earth do you come
up with these incredible story ideas? I
mean, why not throw in some anti-
capitalist, anti-British, anti-f family
messages for good measure? I'm sure
that'll help to ease the crippling guilt
and self-loathing that creeps into the
darkest corners of your minds when
you're lying in bed in the small hours
of the nights.
It's funny, you know, because even
Disney at its worst doesn't openly
despise its own audience quite as much
as the BBC does. It actually feels like
this entire show was conceived and
executed out of pure spite. They knew
the audience hated it, and instead of
trying to improve it, they decided to
just hate their audience right back.
It's a bold strategy, Cotton. Let's see
if it pays off for them. Nice one.
That'll definitely show them. And now
that it's all over, the question on the
minds of the literally dozens of Doctor
Who fans still out there is what happens
next? Well, I can tell you one thing for
free. Billy Piper ain't going to be
leading this show. According to a
longtime leaker known only as Andrew, an
insider who made it his personal mission
to spoil every aspect of this season.
[ __ ] legend. Piper's inclusion was a
lastminute decision on the part of
Russell T. Davis with no real plan of
how she'd be used or where they intended
to go with her. stunt casting, if you
will. And she only did it as a personal
favor, not because she actually wanted
to come back or
anything. Disney aren't likely to pay
for more of this stuff either, because
you can only steal so much money from
the British taxpayer to finance your dog
propaganda. I love paying my license
fee, by the way. I love being legally
compelled to let the British government
take money from my bank account every
month to pay for programming made by
people who actively despise my very
existence. The past two seasons have
actually been propped up by a
co-inancing deal with Disney Plus.
Unfortunately, unlike the weird
crumbling relic of postwar socialist
Britain that is the BBC, Disney are an
actual business that have to make money
from their investments, even if they're
not particularly good at it these days.
and a show that costs tens of millions
of dollars with overnight ratings lower
than most of my YouTube videos isn't
likely to appeal to them. The reality is
nobody's watching this show anymore and
so nobody's willing to pay for more of
it. It's just a burned out husk of what
it once was. All of its creative energy
and fan goodwill has been used up now
and there's nothing left but apathy. And
for guys like Russell and his champagne
socialist chums at the BBC, well, I hope
it was all worth it. I hope you guys
slapped yourselves on the backs extra
hard for this one, knowing that you've
nuked a show with a 60-year legacy,
knowing that you've stood upon the
shoulders of giants and took a dump on
them. The only logical choice now is to
just let Doctor Who die, or at least put
it to sleep for a good long while,
preferably at least a decades. Let
people start to miss it, if that's even
possible at this point, and bring it
back with a new generation of creatives
at the helm. you know, people who
actually care about making a fun family
sci-fi show instead of a political
cudgel to beat the unwashed masses over
the head with. And I guess that's my
real point here. You could put this show
on ice until [ __ ] doomsday and it
wouldn't make the slightest difference
until the culture and mindset that
inflicted this tidal wave of liquefied
feces on the world is purged from the
show. The obsession with identity and
oppression, the constant pandering and
lecturing, the hiring of people for what
they are instead of what they can do,
and the arrogant belief that it's
everyone else who's the problem instead
of the garbage that you're pumping out.
Doctor Who has basically been dead for a
long time now. And now that it seems to
be actually over, maybe it's time to
just let it rest in peace. Now, just
before I benerate my way to the bottom
of another whiskey bottle, I wanted to
let you know about a little project I've
been working on lately. You may have
heard by now, but myself and a few other
creators have partnered up with Ripverse
Comics to put together our own anthology
series, Saints and Sinners. With six
different stories ranging from action to
intrigue to existential horror and over
a 100 pages long, it's a pretty neat
compilation of diverse works from
different authors. and it's rapidly
closing in on 10,000 copies sold
already. It comes with a bunch of
variant covers that you can choose from
and naturally mine is the coolest. So,
if you're interested in taking a look,
the link is in the
description. Anyway, that's all I've got
for
today. Go away now.
